verses.text: 'O God, my heart is fixed; I will sing and give praise,' awaking early, for God's mercy is great 'above the heavens.' His resolve is steady. A settled heart finds endless reason to praise the boundless mercy of God.

'O God, my heart is fixed; I will sing and give praise,' awaking early, for God's mercy is great 'above the heavens.' His resolve is steady. A settled heart finds endless reason to praise the boundless mercy of God.

'O God, my heart is fixed; I will sing and give praise,' awaking early, for God's mercy is great 'above the heavens.' His resolve is steady. A settled heart finds endless reason to praise the boundless mercy of God.

He prays, 'Be thou exalted, O God, above the heavens,' that God's beloved may be delivered by His right hand. His chief desire is God's glory. The believer seeks God's honour first, sure his own rescue will follow.

He prays, 'Be thou exalted, O God, above the heavens,' that God's beloved may be delivered by His right hand. His chief desire is God's glory. The believer seeks God's honour first, sure his own rescue will follow.
God has spoken in His holiness, claiming the lands as His own to divide. His sovereign word stands. The nations and their borders are wholly at the disposal of God.
God has spoken in His holiness, claiming the lands as His own to divide. His sovereign word stands. The nations and their borders are wholly at the disposal of God.
God has spoken in His holiness, claiming the lands as His own to divide. His sovereign word stands. The nations and their borders are wholly at the disposal of God.

Who will bring him into the strong city but God, who had seemed to cast them off? 'Through God we shall do valiantly,' for 'vain is the help of man.' Strength is from God alone. With Him His people overcome; without Him human aid is empty.
